hints.viam: 'viam' is a noun meaning 'way/road' in the accusative
hints.Domini: 'Domini' is a noun meaning 'of the Lord' in the genitive
hints.parare: 'parare' is the infinitive form — 'Parate' is the imperative used here

hints.to appear: 'to appear' is 'apparere' — 'Parate' comes from 'parare' (to prepare/make ready)

hints.Indicative: Indicative would state a fact — 'Parate' is a command (prepare!)
hints.Subjunctive: Subjunctive would express possibility — 'Parate' is a direct command
hints.Infinitive: Infinitive would be 'parare' — 'Parate' is the 2nd person plural imperative

hints.pareo, parere, parui, paritum: These are the parts of 'parere' (to obey)
hints.pario, parere, peperi, partum: These are the parts of 'parere' (to give birth)
hints.parco, parcere, peperci, parsum: These are the parts of 'parcere' (to spare)

hints.Nominative: Nominative would be 'via' — 'viam' with -am ending is accusative
hints.Ablative: Ablative would be 'via' — 'viam' is the accusative direct object

hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'viae' ends in -ae
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'viae' ends in -ae
hints.5th declension: 5th declension has genitive in -ei — 'viae' ends in -ae

hints.Nominative: Nominative would be 'Dominus' — 'Domini' with -i ending is genitive
hints.Dative: Dative would be 'Domino' — 'Domini' is genitive (the way of the Lord)
hints.Vocative: Vocative would be 'Domine' — 'Domini' is genitive singular

translation: Prepare the way of the Lord
